word: G1356 - διοπετής - diopetḗs - dee-op-et'-ace - Which fell down from Jupiter

sections.paragraph:
- Original: διοπετής

- Transliteration: Diopetes

- Phonetic: dee-op-et'-ace

- Definition:

1. fallen from Zeus, i.e. from heaven

2. an image of the Ephesian Artemis which was supposed to have fallen from heaven

- Origin: from the alternate of G2203 and the alternate of G4098

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Adjective

- Strong's: From the alternate of G2203 and the alternate of G4098; sky fallen (that is an aerolite): - which fell down from Jupiter.
